medical_information.doctor: Dr. Lauren K. Bos, Hudsonville Family Medicine
medical_information.allergies_line_1: Ethan: Seasonal allergies (pollen, ragweed). Takes over-the-counter loratadine as needed in spring and early summer. No known drug allergies.
medical_information.allergies_line_2: Lydia: Mild peanut allergy (hives and swelling around lips; no history of anaphylaxis). Carries EpiPen Jr; please avoid sharing snacks that may contain peanuts. Caleb: No known allergies.
medical_information.medication_medical_conditions_line_1: Ethan: Mild exercise-induced asthma. Uses albuterol inhaler (2 puffs) before intense physical activity or if wheezing starts. Last asthma flare-up was in fall 2023 during soccer; no ER visits required.
medical_information.medication_medical_conditions_line_2: Lydia: No daily medications. Needs EpiPen Jr available in case of accidental peanut exposure. Parents will provide labeled EpiPen to group leaders during events and trips.
medical_information.medication_medical_conditions_line_3: Caleb: No chronic medical conditions, no daily medications. All three children are up to date on routine childhood immunizations per pediatrician records.
